stories.text: As an operator, I want the agent to background a long test run and keep working, then be told the result when it finishes, so a twenty-minute suite does not stall the whole session.
As an operator, I want the agent to start a dev server in the background and read its output incrementally so it can drive and debug a live application while the server keeps running.
As an operator, I want the agent to attach a watch ("tell me when this errors") to a noisy CI-style build log so it reacts to failures without wading through raw volume.
As an operator, I want a foreground command that outruns its timeout moved to the background instead of killed, so slow-but-healthy work is never lost to an arbitrary cutoff.
As an AI agent, I want a run_in_background option on bash plus an output-check operation so I can start a process, continue other work, and pull only the new output when I choose.
As an AI agent, I want to be woken with the exit status and output access when a background process completes — even while I am lingering idle — so completions never go unnoticed.
As an AI agent, I want to author a deterministic filter script as a watch on a background process so a match wakes me with just the matching excerpt instead of the full stream.
As an AI agent, I want to list and kill my background processes so nothing keeps running past its usefulness.
